about.intro: I'm Darren Oommen, a student focused on medicine, neuroscience, biomedical science, and data-driven research.
about.description: I'm a high school student building a profile around medicine, neuroscience, healthcare, service, and research. I am learning Python, NumPy, Matplotlib, statistics, and data analysis as research tools for biomedical and public health questions.
about.full:
Hi, I'm Darren Oommen. I'm a high school student interested in biomedical science, neuroscience, medicine, and data analysis.

My main academic direction is medicine and neuroscience. I am especially interested in how research, programming, and statistics can be used to better understand health problems. Right now, I am learning Python, NumPy, Matplotlib, and data analysis with the goal of building stronger research-focused projects.

My interest in medicine is also shaped by personal experience. I help care for my brother, who has autism, which has made me more aware of how neurological and developmental conditions affect families and communities.

I'm the co-founder and Healthcare Lead for MyIntern.net, a student internship platform where I focus primarily on healthcare-related opportunities. I am also volunteering at RWJ and Capital Health during summer 2026.

Outside of academics and healthcare, music is a major part of my life. I have performed as a percussionist in youth orchestra, marching band, indoor percussion, church events, weddings, and live community performances. I also started a drum tutoring and summer camp business.

My goal is to combine medicine, neuroscience, research, service, leadership, and creativity into work that has real-world impact.

roadmap.description:
My coursework is centered around medicine, neuroscience, biomedical science, and research preparation.

Relevant coursework includes Biology, Chemistry Honors, AP Seminar, Pre-Calculus Honors, AP Computer Science Principles, PLTW Introduction to Engineering Design, and dual-enrollment STEM coursework.

I am also self-studying AP Psychology and AP Calculus BC. AP Psychology connects directly to neuroscience and behavior, while AP Calculus BC supports the math foundation needed for statistics, modeling, and computational research.

I use Python, NumPy, Matplotlib, statistics, and data analysis as research tools rather than just general coding skills.

Freshman year at Mercer County Technical Schools Sypek Center through the MCTS STEM Academy.

GPA: 4.44 weighted and 4.00 unweighted.

Coursework included Biology, World History, Spanish I, Algebra II, AP Computer Science Principles, PLTW Introduction to Engineering Design, Physical and Health Education I, and English I.

Dual-enrollment credit came from Mercer County Community College and Rochester Institute of Technology. Courses included MCCC BIO113, MCCC HIS113, MCCC MAT115, MCCC DRA190, and RIT PLTW101.

Notable achievement: scored a 5 on AP Computer Science Principles.

Sophomore year at Hopewell Valley Central High School.

GPA: 4.43 weighted and 4.08 unweighted.

Coursework included AP Seminar, Chemistry Honors, English 2 Honors, Pre-Calculus Honors, Spanish 3, US History 1 Global Honors, Basic Piano, and Wellness 2.

This year helped strengthen my chemistry foundation, math preparation, academic writing, research communication, and overall direction toward medicine and biomedical science.

I self-studied AP Psychology and AP Calculus BC to push beyond my school schedule and connect my interests in neuroscience, math, statistics, and biomedical research.

I am also building research-focused technical skills in Python, NumPy, Matplotlib, statistics, and data analysis.

I earned a Fusion 360 certification, connecting my STEM background to engineering design, digital modeling, and technical problem-solving.

My main interests are biomedical science, neuroscience, medicine, public health, and computational research.

I am especially interested in how programming and statistics can support biomedical questions, public health communication, and neuroscience-related research.

My current technical focus includes Python, NumPy, Matplotlib, statistics, data visualization, and literature review.

I am the co-founder and Healthcare Lead for MyIntern.net, a student internship platform. I focus primarily on finding and organizing healthcare-related opportunities for students interested in medicine, biomedical science, and related fields.

I am currently volunteering at RWJ and Capital Health during summer 2026.

I have also volunteered with special needs children at Princeton Alliance Church during summer Sundays and one-week summer camps in 2024 and 2025.

My experience caring for my brother, who has autism, has made neuroscience and developmental medicine especially meaningful to me.

I serve as Vice President of Model Congress and have been involved in Model United Nations.

At Rutgers Model UN in 2025, I won Best Position Paper in the Disarmament and International Security Committee.

I also served as a Church Lay Leader at Christos Mar Thoma Church in Pennsylvania and managed the choir website during 2024-2025.

Music is a major part of my life, especially percussion and drum set.

I was a GPYO Principal Percussionist for two years and performed at venues including Princeton Richardson Auditorium and Rutgers Mason Gross Performing Arts Center.

I marched bass drum in Nottingham Marching Band, snare drum in Nottingham Indoor Percussion, and tenors at Hopewell Valley Central High School.

I have played drum set and percussion at weddings, church events, Main Street Disneyland, and with Musify Philly at the First FOMAA National Women's Summit, where I was featured on Flowers TV.

I also launched a drum tutoring and summer camp business and currently work with one active student.

Outside of academics, medicine, and music, I enjoy creative and hands-on hobbies.

I earned a poom belt in Taekwondo under two-time national champion Master Park. I also enjoy fishing, juggling, origami, and ventriloquism.

I previously created an origami and ventriloquism YouTube channel that reached 52 subscribers, with the highest-viewed video reaching 522 views.
